title: Earthy Eggless Thai Curry Khao Soi
description: Sour, bouncy-qq, sick-day Thai dish featuring beer, papillote, egg-free-friendly. Made with Central Thai flavors and copper pots for authenticity.

ingredients.name: Thai red curry paste
ingredients.notes: Make sure to get a brand without eggs

ingredients.name: Beer ( Thai beer preferred )
ingredients.notes: Not necessary to brew a big batch, just grab a bottle from the fridge

instructions: Heat oil in the copper pot over medium-high heat. Then add onion and cook until it starts to brown, about 3 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, and a pinch of salt. Stir until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add curry paste, stir for 1 minute, getting everything coated evenly.
Add coconut milk and beer. Stir well and bring the mixture to boil.
Add tamarind paste, palm sugar, and fish sauce (if using). Stir well and continue to boil for 5 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Add noodles and stir well to combine.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the noodles are heated through and coated in the sauce.
Garnish with Thai basil leaves and a squeeze of lime juice. Serve hot, steaming into small paper cups if desired.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Let the dish rest for a minute or two before serving.
To serve, place a generous portion of noodles and sauce into a shallow copper pot, and garnish with fresh Thai basil leaves and a lime wedge on the side.
Be careful when eating this dish as the noodles are hot and could cause burns if mishandled.
